Results for VDBA Scaling Analysis

O.W.

2025-11-10

Comapre 5 seconds and 1 second windows

To demonstarte that these are interchangable and results not affected by the length of the window

Only Datasets With Long Data

Maybe some of the small datasets are skewing it… Exclude datasets that had less than 24 hrs worth of data.

Single system analysis

What is the differences in sampling protocol are throwing it out? Collected only data collected in the same way by the same lab. # V1: Clemente There were insufficint individuals, so removed ID as an effect and just had a linear model. 

## # A tibble: 4 × 7
##   term                      estimate std.error statistic  p.value sig   variable
##   <chr>                        <dbl>     <dbl>     <dbl>    <dbl> <chr> <chr>   
## 1 LogMass                    -0.336     0.0801    -4.20   4.07e-5 sig   mean    
## 2 LogMass:thresholdall       -0.0428    0.113     -0.378  7.06e-1 not … mean    
## 3 LogMass:thresholdinactive   0.322     0.113      2.84   4.96e-3 sig   mean    
## 4 LogMass                    -0.0965    0.0802    -1.20   2.33e-1 not … max

V2: Buchmann

Similarly ID was not a random effect here as too few individuals. 

## # A tibble: 4 × 7
##   term                      estimate std.error statistic  p.value sig   variable
##   <chr>                        <dbl>     <dbl>     <dbl>    <dbl> <chr> <chr>   
## 1 LogMass                   -0.489       0.164   -2.98   0.00383  sig   mean    
## 2 LogMass:thresholdall      -0.00266     0.232   -0.0115 0.991    not … mean    
## 3 LogMass:thresholdinactive  0.605       0.408    1.48   0.142    not … mean    
## 4 LogMass                   -0.602       0.160   -3.75   0.000614 sig   max

Specific Masses

Pooled

Maybe the species masses are too vauge… what if we only use the datasets where we know the exact mass? Again, just a linear model with no random effect for ID. 

## # A tibble: 4 × 7
##   term                      estimate std.error statistic p.value sig    variable
##   <chr>                        <dbl>     <dbl>     <dbl>   <dbl> <chr>  <chr>   
## 1 LogMass                    -0.233     0.0786    -2.96  0.00345 sig    mean    
## 2 LogMass:thresholdall       -0.0254    0.111     -0.228 0.820   not s… mean    
## 3 LogMass:thresholdinactive   0.149     0.111      1.34  0.182   not s… mean    
## 4 LogMass                     0.0795    0.104      0.763 0.448   not s… max

Even further separated

Where the masses of the exact individual are known. Per species plot and then all known individuals on the same graph.
